rationale: The cited paper is used in the citing paper's Dynamic WOM Effectiveness section to support two core premises: WOM is dynamically formed and WOM effects on outcomes evolve over time. Those premises directly motivate a central part of the citing model, where WOM effectiveness is explicitly time-varying and linked to ad-WOM interdependence across distribution stages. Although the citation appears in grouped references and the citing paper does not reuse the cited paper's exact hazard/decomposition method, it still plays a meaningful conceptual framing role for the model specification.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Moe and Trusov (2011) as empirical/conceptual support that online WOM exhibits temporal social dynamics and dynamic outcome effects. It then builds its own framework by modeling dynamic WOM effectiveness in a different setting (sequential theater-to-video distribution with advertising interaction).
